Plastic pollution poses severe threats to marine life and ecosystems across the globe. Many animals inadvertently ingest floating debris, which causes internal blockages and malnutrition. Additionally, plastics break down into microplastics that enter the food web, accumulating in tissues and potentially impacting human health through seafood consumption. Coastal environments suffer from reduced water quality due to runoff carrying pollutants. Coral reefs, mangroves, and seagrass beds are particularly vulnerable to chemical leaching and physical damage from tangled objects. Over time, these disruptions compromise the ecological balance, reducing the resilience of natural habitats against other stressors like climate change. Protecting the ocean requires global efforts to reduce waste and improve recycling infrastructure.<|im_end|>
